page: OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a GPU-accelerated instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon W-3235 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores operating at 3.3 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 84.0 GB of RAM, providing 5.25 GB of memory per core. For hardware acceleration, it integrates three NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPUs based on the Turing architecture, delivering a combined 48 GB of GDDR6 VRAM. Local storage consists of a 400 GB SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in specialized parallel workloads. The multi-GPU configuration enables top-tier LLM inference speeds, achieving 1827.36 tokens per second for prompt processing on 7B models and 220.48 tokens per second on 70B models. Text generation speeds reach 65.53 tokens per second for 7B models. CPU performance is solid, with a Passmark CPU score of 20742.83 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 22027.17, placing it in the average tier. Memory performance is highlighted by a strong Passmark memory score of 2913.07, though large-block uncached memory write speeds represent a relative bottleneck at 25.38 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the rtx5000-84 offers a balanced hardware profile for multi-GPU workloads without the high cost associated with newer enterprise-class accelerators. The provision of three dedicated GPUs makes it highly suitable for parallelized tasks. This server is optimized for workloads such as large language model inference, ray tracing, image processing, and parallelized CUDA-based computations.
description: The OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring an Intel Xeon W-3235 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores running at 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 84.0 GB of system memory and a 400 GB local SSD. The core differentiator is its triple NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPU configuration, providing a total of 48 GB of VRAM. This setup delivers top-tier performance in LLM inference, achieving 1827.36 tokens per second for prompt processing on 7B models. While memory write speeds for large uncached blocks are relatively weak at 25.38 GB/sec, the server excels in parallelized graphics and compute tasks. It represents a cost-efficient option for multi-GPU workloads, making it highly suitable for machine learning inference, ray tracing, and data science pipelines.
og_description: OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 16 Intel Xeon cores, 84 GB RAM, a 400 GB SSD, and three NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPUs delivering 48 GB of total VRAM for parallelized workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a KVM-based GPU server with 16 Intel Xeon cores, 84 GB RAM, and three NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPUs for LLM inference and graphics.
tagline: A triple-GPU accelerated server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 48 GB of total VRAM for parallelized compute and inference workloads.
bullet_points: Three NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPUs providing 48 GB of total VRAM.
Intel Xeon W-3235 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores at 3.3 GHz.
84.0 GB of system memory and 400 GB of local SSD storage.
Top-tier LLM inference performance for small, medium, and large models.
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one complimentary public IPv4 address.
Best suited for machine learning inference, ray tracing, and parallel CUDA tasks.
